terms.term: Authority The formally assigned right to make, override, pause, or stop decisions during execution.
Decision Boundary The explicit point at which authority transfers between actors during execution.
Stop Authority A role-bound capability to halt system operation when governance thresholds are exceeded.
Escalation Transfer of decision authority to higher roles when risk thresholds are reached.
Escalation Decay Reduction in the effectiveness or use of escalation pathways over time.
Escalation Suppression Condition where escalation pathways exist but are not used.
Behavioral Drift Gradual divergence between expected system behavior and observed behavior over time.
Reliance Formation Process through which individuals begin depending on system outputs.
Governance as Infrastructure Governance structures that accumulate through repeated behavioral patterns.
Continuous Assurance Ongoing monitoring of governance performance rather than periodic audits.
Longitudinal Accountability Traceability of authority and responsibility across time. --- ## GOV — Governance Mechanisms and Accountability
Accountability Binding outcomes to named roles with authority prior to execution.
Accountability Diffusion Loss of clear responsibility caused by distributed decision processes.
Responsibility Binding Mechanism linking authority to responsible actors.
Decision Substitution Replacement of human decision-making with automated system outputs. --- ## RISK — Longitudinal Risk and Failure Dynamics
Governance Lag Delay between behavioral change and governance response.
Governance Drift Misalignment between governance policy and operational behavior.
Longitudinal Risk Risk emerging through repeated behavioral patterns over time.
Behavioral Accumulation Compounding effect of repeated actions reshaping system behavior.
Governance Failure Breakdown of governance mechanisms controlling system behavior.
Authority Drift Gradual shift in decision authority away from intended structures.
Judgment Externalization Transfer of interpretive responsibility from humans to automated systems.
Confidence Reinforcement Feedback loop where system responses increase user confidence.
Override Erosion Decline in willingness to override automated decisions.
Normalization of Workarounds Temporary fixes gradually becoming accepted operational practices.
Governance Illusion Perception of governance control without effective enforcement. --- ## MET — Measurement Constructs
Language Symmetry Score (LSS) Metric measuring alignment between system outputs and governance terminology.
Relational Rhythm Index (RRI) Indicator measuring stability in human-system interaction patterns.
Governance Stability Index (GSI) Metric measuring long-term consistency of governance structures.
Authority Alignment Score (AAS) Metric evaluating alignment between authority structures and governance design. --- ## OPS — Operational Monitoring Systems
Governance Telemetry Operational signals measuring governance performance.
Interaction Trace Recorded sequence of human-system interactions. --- ## SYS — Structural Governance Systems
Sociotechnical System System composed of human, technical, and organizational components.
Execution-Time Governance Governance mechanisms operating during system execution.
Governance Infrastructure Layer Structural components enforcing governance rules.
